sourceMovie.title: In Bruges
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto In Bruges (2008) as a comedy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Filth
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If In Bruges clicked for you, Filth (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after In Bruges?
faq.answer: Try Filth, God Bless America, Blindspotting—each shares DNA with In Bruges while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is In Bruges?
faq.answer: In Bruges (2008) is best described as Comedy, Drama, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is In Bruges worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
